Hi @JohnF17, the Scrollbar does not know about the scrollPadding your are providing to the TextField. The RawScrollbar allows you to apply padding and margins, maybe we should expose those properties on the Scrollbar subclass.

> the Scrollbar does not know about the scrollPadding your are providing to the TextField.

Hi @Piinks, that is true indeed, forgot to mention that in the OP, I was going to mention how they have no way to know about eachother unless the `ScrollController` helps link them in that regard. 

1. What would the solution look like at the end of it? Even though we can fix this by adding some padding to the right of the scrollbar, design-wise it might not look good with all that space its leaving just to make way for the curved edges and look as if its fixed the clipping. 
Not that I know best but putting the TextField typing area and the scrollbar in a row within a container like arrangement I believe would address this better as it would force the scrollbar to be within the bounds of the container no matter the border radius. 🤷‍♂️ (Like the scrollbar behaviour you see here on github issue TextField)

2. Why would `Textfield.scrollPadding` not work as it should while its **inside** the `TextField` but `RawScrollBar.padding` work while its just parenting it? what's even the use of `Textfield.scrollPadding` then because it seems to do nothing 👀

Hope these are valid questions and help get the issue fixed ASAP 🙏 as it looks bad for users, till the issue gets addressed I'll keep it hidden or remove the top-right border radius only. Thank You.

P.s [Proposal] - Not related to the issue
- Arrows on the scrollbar (maybe a package)
- Ability to resize the textfield box with a drag icon

I am not sure what a solution would look like yet. The scrollPadding of TextField does not relate or get passed on to any of the scrollable parts of the TextField, it just applies space to the content within the TextField, it does not actually relate to scrolling at all so I don't know that the scrollbar can automatically discern this information in any way.

> TextField typing area and the scrollbar in a row within a container like arrangement

Since the scrollbar in your example code is outside of the TextField I don't know if that could work. Have you looked at ScrollBehavior.buildScrollbar? Rather than directly applying a scrollbar outside of the TextField, the inherited ScrollBehavior could apply it internally instead.

I am not sure I understand the `2.` point above. 
 
---

> P.s [Proposal] - Not related to the issue
> 
> Arrows on the scrollbar (maybe a package)
> Ability to resize the textfield box with a drag icon

I think issues for these already exist, if not please file separate issues since, as you said, they do not relate to this one.

issues.title: TextField edit menu position problem
issues.body:
When there is a lot of text in the TextField, the actual height of the text exceeds the TextField a lot,   so, if I select all the text, an edit menu will pop up, similar to 'copy / cut / select all'.

But the position of the edit menu will be far away from the TextField, and displayed on the first line of the actual height of the text. 

anybody can help please?

In the recent repro https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/56176#issuecomment-1497319539, it looks like the behavior is greatly improved compared to the original report and the earlier repros (from 2020) above.

In particular, in the recent repro:
* The text selection toolbar is always visible, even when we're scrolled to the middle of the selected text so that neither the start nor end of the text is in view.
* The text selection toolbar is always located immediately next to the TextField (and in fact next to the selected text that's visible in the TextField), never far away from it like it is in the original report and in one of the earlier screenshots.

There's still a difference from iOS native behavior:
* In the Flutter app, the text selection toolbar appears just above the text.
* In the native iOS app, it sometimes appears in the *middle* of the text.

I guess one thing I'm curious about is:
* In the native iOS app, it looks like it prefers to put the selection toolbar above or below the text, and it only uses the middle when the text occupies the whole viewport so that there's no room above or below.
* In that test Flutter app, there's always room above the text.
* What does Flutter do when the selected text occupies the whole viewport so that there's no room above or below?

issues.title: [macOS] keyboard Exception on start app while caps lock is on
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. create a Flutter demo app.
2. add a text field on the demo page.
3. turn on the caps lock on the keypad.
4. run the app for the target platform macOS.
5. try to use the keypad in the text field.
6. see the logs will find an exception for Caps Lock in app init.

### Expected results

FieldText accepts keyboard press buttons and events.


### Actual results

 An unhandled exception is thrown in App startup and any keyboard events are not listened to.

issues.title: TextFormField doesn't render its border as said in the docs.
issues.body:
I think there's a problem in the way `TextFormField` deals with its border.

As far as I understand from the docs, the `border` parameter will take an `InputDecoration` shape and will substitute the color and weight from the others parameters like `enabledBorder` and `focusedBorder`.

So if this is true, the following code should work:

```
TextFormField(
  decoration: InputDecoration(
    enabledBorder: const OutlineInputBorder(
      borderSide: const BorderSide(color: Colors.orange),
     ) ,
     focusedBorder: const OutlineInputBorder(
       borderSide: const BorderSide(color: Colors.white),
     ) ,
     border: OutlineInputBorder(
       borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(100)
     )
  ),
)
```

In this code I define a border shape where corners are rounded and **ONLY** the color of the border changes if it's enabled or focused.

But in fact what I am seeing is that the shape in `border` is being totally ignored and the `TextFormField` is rendered as a square with orange or white colors, no rounded edges.

The only way I can get it to work is with this very repetitive code:

```
TextFormField(
  decoration: InputDecoration(
    enabledBorder: OutlineInputBorder(
	  borderSide: BorderSide(color: Colors.orange),
	  borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(100)
	) ,
	focusedBorder: OutlineInputBorder(
	  borderSide: BorderSide(color: Colors.white),
	  borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(100)
	) ,
	border: OutlineInputBorder(
	  borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(100)
	)
  ),
)
```

Is this really the correct way to achieive this?

I tried @HansMuller short test case here: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/19694#issuecomment-469376553 but since it doesn't have a rounded border it doesn't match what I see. I think the problem lies in `borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(100)`.
comments:
OutlineInputBorder's borderRadius parameter defaults to `BorderRaidus.circular(4)` so there's no avoiding providing that parameter for each InputDecoration border. This version of the test case appears to do what you want: https://gist.github.com/HansMuller/7b9c44ee67e9fa260678f423af6350bd, albeit with a smaller border radius.

![border](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1377460/53825805-05cde000-3f2c-11e9-9d4b-4f0763a239a2.png)

That said, I can see two real problems here:
- Using large border radius values (like 100) breaks the trig code that computes the focused border's gap. 
- I assume that what you're looking for is a "stadium" border outline. What we probably need for that case is a `StadiumInputBorder` class.

> so there's no avoiding providing that parameter for each InputDecoration border.

Well this is the case then I think the docs should be modified, the way I understood them was that the border would be maintained as defined by `boder` and only color and minor things would change. Maybe what will change and what will not should be better documented?

>Using large border radius values (like 100) breaks the trig code that computes the focused border's gap.

Yes, I saw some weird behavior using the label, so I am only using `hint`.

> I assume that what you're looking for is a "stadium" border outline. What we probably need for that case is a StadiumInputBorder class.

Yes, I want a "stadium" border outline and created an issue for it here #28611 already.

```

    border: UnderlineInputBorder(),

```

According to code, there are some situations:

- if your TextField's `enabled:` is **_false_**, 
-- then work **`disabledBorder:`** ,  
--- there is **NO FOCUS!** in this situation, because it is disabled.

- if your TextField's `enabled:` is **_true_**, 
-- then work **`enabledBorder:`** ,  
--- there is a **FOCUS!** 
---- when i focus TextField, then work **`focusedBorder:`** .

- if you have an error (or you fill `errorText:` in your TextField), 
-- then work **`errorBorder:`** , 
--- there is a **FOCUS!** 
---- when i focus TextField, **i expect `focusedBorder:` will work** again. BUT NOT WORKING

**Advices:**

1. InputDecoration class need an **`errorFocusedBorder:`** parameter and don't need `border:` parameter. 
It's just a mess.

2. `focusedBorder:` parameter should change or deprecate with **`enabledFocusedBorder:`** parameter.

Hi @ruialves35, welcome to Flutter. I will try to answer some of your questions:

1. In a nutshell, the code that draws a `TextField`'s border is not simple, there are parts that are kinda more "low level", with pixel calculations (to draw the lines and curves) and all of the parameters to decide how to draw them, come from another "place". I am simplifying things here, just so you understand that's not a quick fix.
2. Stadium is the shape of a curved square, like a football stadium shape. The idea is to have a new and more customisable shape that we can plug-in there.
3. The reason why this has not being done yet is probably because there are other higher priority tasks do be done.
4. Be careful when asking questions regarding time estimates or why things are not done. They are not welcome in the community, they generate unnecessary friction with the Flutter team, as they have lots of stuff to do. This kind of question can get you banned, take a look at the community guidelines please.

issues.title: Text Field obscure text cannot be used with  expands to true
issues.body:
Putting an assert to check whether the text is a single line if it is obscured is a good idea when we think first. However, when we do like that we also avoid the use of "expands" property. For example, if I create a login screen with a password text form field wrapped with an expanded widget, I cannot fill the full size inside of the expanded with a text form field. The reason why I can't do it is the fact that obscured texts cannot be multiline and max lines should be null (cannot be 1) when expands is true. These two asserts lead to the problem and this problem leads to significant design issues especially when we are dealing with websites developed with Flutter Web since their size can be any.

Like it is mentioned in:

"the problem is that if i try to use a text field (obscure) that occupies the entire space of a container using Expands: true and max Lines: null, it will not work, only the text without obscure now.. :/"
_Originally posted by @FilipeAG in https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/41853#issuecomment-614040451_

Use this code piece in any project to reproduce the issue:
```
Expanded(
        child: TextFormField(
        controller: controller, // Declare above
        obscureText: true,
      ),
      );
```

When you use the above code, TextFormField doesn't fill the all space of its parent since the obscured text form fields are forced to be "one" line. If we want to use the "expands" parameter of TextFormField as true, it doesn't pass the following assert:
```
 assert(
         !expands || (maxLines == null && minLines == null),
         'minLines and maxLines must be null when expands is true.',
       ),
```
If we try to set minLines and maxLines as null, then it doesn't pass the following assert:
`assert(!obscureText || maxLines == 1, 'Obscured fields cannot be multiline.'),`

So each assert leads to failure in another assert like the links of a chain.
In short: "How can we create an **obscured** TextFormField that **expands** the parent widget?"

cc: @justinmc

Yes I think this is working as intended, since an obscured TextField can't be multiline.  @bahricanyesil What's the end result you want?  A tall text field with a single line of obscured text up at the top?

> Yes I think this is working as intended, since an obscured TextField can't be multiline.  @bahricanyesil What's the end result you want? A tall text field with a single line of obscured text up at the top?

When I use email and password text form fields wrapped with expanded widget with same flex, email field can expand itself according to the screen size but password field cannot due to this restriction. This leads to an inconsistency in the design. While email text field's height is changing when I change the window size on chrome, the other one not.

The expected result, being able to use both obscured and not obscured text form fields wrapped with expanded widget and get the same result. So, as you said at the end of your sentence: "A tall text field with a single line of obscured text" but without giving a certain height.

Yh, that's exactly what I meant. As I said we can find a workaround by setting dynamic "height" values. However, I think it will be better if we can use expand feature in obscured text fields too.

One downside of setting the email field to a single line is the fact that it will force the widget to have a small size.
What I meant with code examples and screenshots:
This is the expected result that allocates half of the screen height to both text form fields:

![expected_result](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/60237280/135474831-83ac9869-b405-4b33-add7-a5c340ff945f.png)

This is what we get when we have to use obscureText as true and so have to set the maxLines field to "1" (as a result of this we cannot use expands parameter as true)

![have_to_get](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/60237280/135474867-83f908a1-2136-475a-b0ef-78bd65dae420.png)

Ok I see your point... When I designed this it didn't cross my mind that this would be useful.

If I just allowed `expands` to be used with any combination of `minLines` and `maxLines`, then that would allow things like this (sized based on the container):

![why_not](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/389558/135662040-50fce9b7-7249-451d-86c9-fda0b8ffde98.gif)

Is that useful to allow in addition to the obscured expands field?

I am not sure whether this will be useful or not. However, for specific-sized containers, it can be useful anyways. I think, allowing flexibility and giving this feature to the users may be beneficial in some cases that I cannot give an example of now. My main point was the obscured expands field.

Hi @cedvdb, thanks for reporting this issue. Currently `EditableText` does not support the full-suite of text selection gestures on any platform, it only supports tap and long press gestures. Input/Text widgets that support the full-suite of text selection are `TextField/CupertinoTextField/SelectableText`. To give `EditableText` gesture support you need to use [`TextSelectionGestureDetectorBuilder`](https://api.flutter.dev/flutter/widgets/TextSelectionGestureDetectorBuilder-class.html) which builds a `TextSelectionGestureDetector` that you can wrap your `EditableText` with. Let me know if that solves your issue.
@Renzo-Olivares I used Textfield instead.
Thanks for the fyi - noting this as another issue that https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/120613 would resolve (for web)

issues.title: [iOS] Password save dialog not appearing with autofillHints on Flutter 3.27.1
issues.body:
### Description
While the password save dialog was displayed using autofillHints in Flutter 3.10.5, upgrading to Flutter 3.27.1 caused the same code to no longer display the password save dialog. Please check why it is no longer appearing.

### Steps to reproduce

1．Install the app for the first time.
2．Enter ID/password.
3．The password save dialog does not appear.

### Flutter Version Comparison
Version where it worked: Flutter 3.10.5
Version where the issue occurs: Flutter 3.27.1

### Reproduction Environment
iOS Device: iPhone 11, iOS 18.2
Xcode Version: 15.3
CocoaPods: 1.16.2

### Expected results

The password save dialog should appear after entering the password.

### Actual results

The password save dialog does not appear even after entering the password.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
Widget _passwordField(BuildContext ctx) {
    final obscure = ctx.select<LoginState, bool>((state) => state.obscure);
    final validity = ctx.select<LoginState, bool>((state) => state.passwordValidity);
    return Column(
      crossAxisAlignment: CrossAxisAlignment.start,
      children: [
        _title(AppKey().l10n!.password),
        Padding(
          padding: const EdgeInsets.only(bottom: 10, left: 30, right: 30),
          child: ConstrainedBox(
            constraints: const BoxConstraints(maxWidth: itemMaxSize),
            child: Visibility(
              visible: ctx.select<LoginState, bool>((state) => state.isMain),
              child: TextFormField(
                textInputAction: TextInputAction.done,
                controller: ctx.read<LoginStateNotifier>().passwordController,
                decoration: InputDecoration(
                  border: const OutlineInputBorder(),
                  fillColor: errorBackgroundColor,
                  filled: !validity,
                  suffixIcon: IconButton(
                    icon: Icon(
                      obscure ? Icons.visibility_off : Icons.visibility,
                      color: validity ? Colors.grey : Colors.red,
                    ),
                    onPressed: ctx.read<LoginStateNotifier>().onChangeObscure,
                  ),
                  counterText: '',
                ),
                obscureText: obscure,

                onEditingComplete: TextInput.finishAutofillContext,
                maxLength: 50,
                keyboardType: TextInputType.visiblePassword,
                onChanged: ctx.read<LoginStateNotifier>().onChangePassword,
                validator: ctx.read<LoginStateNotifier>().validatePassword,
              ),
            ),
          ),
        ),
      ],
    );
  }

issues.title: [iOS] TextInputType breaks previous focus
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

- Create two or more TextFields within the same column, once with keyboardType = TextInputType.number or TextInputType.phone.

- Navigate backwards using keyboard navigation (Shift+TAB) without release the Shift button.



### Expected results

Keeping Shift key pressed, every hit on TAB button moves the focus on the previous TextField.

### Actual results

Keeping Shift key pressed, every hit on TAB button moves the focus on the previous TextField. Whenever reaches the one with keyboard type, it starts to move the focus on the next TextField instead of the previous.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
void main() {
  runApp(
    MaterialApp(
      home: Scaffold(
        body: SafeArea(
          child: Column(
            children: [
              TextField(),
              TextField(
                keyboardType: TextInputType.number,
              ),
              TextField(),
            ],
          ),
        ),
      ),
    ),
  );
}

@tommaso2212 Thanks for the details report.
@darshankawar Thanks for triaging this issue.

I confirm that this issue is reproducible on iOS and only for `TextField` with `keyboardType` sets to  `TextInputType.number` or `TextInputType.phone`.

Especially, this description from https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/151312#issuecomment-2213438287 is important :

> If that helps, the problem occurs only if keeping "Shift" key pressed while hitting "TAB" multiple times.

While investigating this I noticed that the framework received a synthesized key up event for the shift key despite the key still being pressed (which leads to the shortcut resolution opting for `NextFocusAction` instead of `PreviousFocusAction`).

I updated the labels to mark this as an engine issue and I will try to get some logs from the engine side.

Good catch!

@dkwingsmt Sorry I always tag you when I see something related to keyboard events. And this is iOS. But if you have any thoughts off the top of your head on why the we'd get a keyUp for shift let us know.

Hi @hatano0x06 thanks for reporting this issue! I will try to clear up some of the behavior observed above.

For all platforms except Android, we prevent coalescing during CJK composition. This behavior seems to be consistent with native platform behavior, see https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/106473 and https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/108765 for more context on the motivation behind this implementation. This means that doing `undo` while composing will result in the state being returned to what it was before the composing began.

In the video above for the Japanese language on iOS you can see that when `undo` is pressed the composition is still active as indicated by the solid underline which is why `undo` does nothing. Though thinking about this some more I think it is expected that `undo` in this case would result in an empty field. What may be happening here is that the initial state of the `TextField` is actively composing since the language is initially CJK, and therefore the initial state is not being saved to the history stack. The same explanation holds true for the Chinese language video on iOS.

For Android we choose to coalesce every edit because Gboard has an active composing region even when composing english text. If we didn't then Undo/Redo would not work at all on Android.

In the Android-English language video, it appears to work correctly because the text is never `confirmed` (as indicated by the solid underline), so all the states saved to the history stack include an active composing region so the user never experiences a `blink`. If we `confirm` the text in between edits then that will result in unexpected behaviors.

The `blink` happens because when the text is confirmed a new history entry is pushed to the stack that does not have an active composing region. When we `undo` and move the history back to the previous entry with the active composing region this causes a visual `blink`.

What happens in the Android-Japanese/Chinese language videos is the same issue described for the english language above plus the Android embedder seems to be resetting the composing region to `(-1, -1)` after our undo action adding another unexpected entry to the history stack which causes the blinking between undos. The embedder does this when the framework has changed the composing region, which happens we execute our undo action.

I am currently working on some improvements to `UndoHistory` so I will keep this issue in mind.

Thank you for your detailed explanation.
The explanation about flashing was easy to understand and I was satisfied!

I look forward to future improvements.
Thank you

              RawKeyboardListener(

This code used to work with v1.10.15, but since v1.10.16 the `RawKeyboardListener` does not receive the key events when the `TextField` is focused.

I've been futzing with it to use keyboard to control ScrollBar - ScrollView to bandaid bug here: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/43694 but after our efforts, got stuck with this. issues.title: [Android]WidgetsBindingObserver.didChangeAppLifecycleState is triggered by selecting an Autofill option
issues.body:
## Issue When I select an autofill option from the keyboard in Android (see screenshot), `WidgetsBindingObserver.didChangeAppLifecycleState` is triggered. The application state shouldn't change because an input has been inserted into a TextField.
Screenshot
Code sample - [Download the sample app](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/files/8168727/textfield_bug.zip). This is just a `flutter create my_app` listening to WidgetsBindingObserver. - Run with a Android device/emulator with Autofill activated (on Android: Settings -> Password & accounts -> Autofill service) - Tap on the TextField and select a candidate (see 1st screenshot) - `WidgetsBindingObserver.didChangeAppLifecycleState()` is triggered (a print is done on the console in `_MyHomePageState` class)

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({Key? key}) : super(key: key);

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(
        primarySwatch: Colors.blue,
      ),
      home: const MyHomePage(title: 'Flutter Demo Home Page'),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyHomePage({Key? key, required this.title}) : super(key: key);

  final String title;

  @override
  State<MyHomePage> createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> with WidgetsBindingObserver {
  bool lifecycleChanged = false;
  List<AppLifecycleState> states = [];

  @override
  void initState() {
    super.initState();
    WidgetsBinding.instance?.addObserver(this);
  }

  @override
  void dispose() {
    WidgetsBinding.instance?.removeObserver(this);
    super.dispose();
  }

  @override
  void didChangeAppLifecycleState(AppLifecycleState state) {
    super.didChangeAppLifecycleState(state);
    print(
        'didChangeAppLifecycleState is triggered from Android keyboard event');

    setState(() {
      lifecycleChanged = true;
      states.add(state);
    });
  }

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(
        title: Text(widget.title),
      ),
      body: Center(
        child: Column(
          mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
          children: <Widget>[
            const TextField(
              style: TextStyle(color: Colors.red),
              obscureText: false,
              autocorrect: true,
              decoration: InputDecoration(
                labelText: "Test field",
                hintText: 'example@address.com',
                errorText: 'Wrong try again',
              ),
              keyboardType: TextInputType.emailAddress,
              textCapitalization: TextCapitalization.none,
              autofillHints: [AutofillHints.email],
              maxLines: 1,
              enableSuggestions: true,
            ),
            if (lifecycleChanged)
              Expanded(
                child: Column(
                  children: [
                    const Text("LifeCycle changed"),
                    Expanded(
                      child: ListView(
                        children: states.map((state) {
                          return Text(state.toString());
                        }).toList(),
                      ),
                    )
                  ],
                ),
              ),
          ],
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}

```

issues.title: TextField is "shifting" when the fontSize is bigger than its constrained height.
issues.body:
## Steps to Reproduce Hi thanks for Flutter! I encounter a problem as follows: When the constraints (maxHeight) of `TextField` is smaller than the text size (or text line height), TextField will *shift everything up* so *the upper half cannot receive touch events and the lower half cannot be rendered*, which is quite abnormal. See the screenshots for what is happening :) 1. Run `flutter create bug`. 2. Update the files as follows: ``` import 'package:flutter/material.dart'; void main() => runApp(MyApp()); class MyApp extends StatelessWidget { @override Widget build(BuildContext context) => MaterialApp(home: MyHomePage()); } class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget { MyHomePage({Key key}) : super(key: key); @override _MyHomePageState createState() => _MyHomePageState(); } class _MyHomePageState extends State { @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return Scaffold( appBar: AppBar(title: Text('Bug!')), body: Column(children: [ Container(height: 150, color: Colors.blue), ConstrainedBox( constraints: BoxConstraints(maxHeight: 100), child: TextField( style: TextStyle( fontSize: 130, ), controller: TextEditingController(text: 'ABCDEFGHIJKLMN'), maxLines: null, ), ), Container(height: 150, color: Colors.green), ])); } } ``` Look at screenshot **Expected results:** Should work normally. See the first paragraph of this issue. **Actual results:** See these screenshots. issues.title: Ellipsis not working properly when a text overflows via constrained height instead of max lines
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new Flutter project
2. Paste the code sample into `main.dart`
3. Run on any platform

### Expected results

Ellipsis should show up at the end of the text if the text is cut off by its parent's constrained height, even if `maxLines` is null or a large value.

### Actual results

Ellipsis shows up when `maxLines` is null, but then it trims the text into one line. Ellipsis does not show at all if `maxLines` is greater than what could normally fit in the constrained size.

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() => runApp(const App());

class App extends StatelessWidget {
  const App({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      home: ExamplePage(),
    );
  }
}

class ExamplePage extends StatelessWidget {
  const ExamplePage({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    final text =
        'Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris nisi ut aliquip ex ea commodo consequat. Duis aute irure dolor in reprehenderit in voluptate velit esse cillum dolore eu fugiat nulla pariatur. Excepteur sint occaecat cupidatat non proident, sunt in culpa qui officia deserunt mollit anim id est laborum.';

    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(title: Text('Example')),
      body: Center(
        child: Column(
          spacing: 16,
          mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
          children: [
            Text('Max lines: null'),
            Container(
              width: 200,
              height: 200,
              color: Colors.blue,
              child: Text(
                text,
                overflow: TextOverflow.ellipsis,
                maxLines: null,
              ),
            ),
            Text('Max lines: 100'),
            Container(
              width: 200,
              height: 200,
              color: Colors.blue,
              child: Text(
                text,
                overflow: TextOverflow.ellipsis,
                maxLines: 100,
              ),
            ),
          ],
        ),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

</details>

issues.title: Command+Enter on Chrome causes Enter Key to be considered held down

I've just tried this on MacOS desktop and this behavior is not reproduced.
It seems as if it's unable to find the corresponding Intent
Flutter seems to be treating the enter key as stuck down. Upon triggering the shortcut again, the held keys includes enter, even though it hasn't been pressed in a while.
More data here: when I press the enter key again, it becomes "unstuck" and the shortcut works again.
I've tried this with another keyboard and confirmed that this happens with a standard USB keyboard.
Using MacOS's Accessibility keyboard, the system itself doesn't seem to think that the enter key is still held down.
I can see from logging in my early Key Event Handler that when meta is held down, no Enter Up event is dispatched; seems like a bug... 🤔

Something seems to be filtering out the enter up key event at a very low level. I've traced the events back to `handleKeyData` in services/hardware_keyboard.dart and the corresponding key up event does not seem to be generated when holding down the meta key. Interestingly, it does seem like the `RawKeyUpEvent` *is* getting generated so it seems like it should be possible for Flutter to generate the corresponding `KeyUpEvent`

@AlabasterAxe I tried the same using latest master and stable running on web platform with mac desktop. Upon pressing `shift + K`, it properly showed me `shortcut triggered` as below: But if I first use `cmd + return` followed by `shift + k`, then the shortcut isn't triggered. I had to hit `return` again then followed by `shift + k` which then resulted in shortcut being triggered. I verified this on macOS desktop as well, in which, performing `cmd + return` followed by `shift + K` does trigger the shortcut properly, so this seems to be web specific issue. ``` Stable : 3.24.2 Master: 3.26.0-1.0.pre.72 ```

Also, I tried this on windows and this doesn't happen (not to mention that an application defining a meta shortcut on windows would be unusual in my experience), so this seems to be specific to Web on Mac 🙃.
This happens on Safari as well so it's possible that MacOS itself is not transmitting that key event...

I added a `keyup` listener to the browser and that is also not getting triggered and I've repeated that across safari and other websites as well so this doesn't seem like something Flutter can do much about. I feel Flutter has been exonorated for this issue :) Feel free to close.
I've filed: https://issues.chromium.org/issues/366284864 against Chrome to try to gather more information.
FYI @dkwingsmt for the relation to keyboard, though it seems that there is now a Chromium bug that this will wait on.

I haven't looked into why the Enter key is considered held down, but I'll suggest replacing `LogicalKeySet` with `SingleActivator`. The former compares whether all keys being held is exactly expected, while the latter implements the "typical shortcuts" correctly by only checking if the modifier keys are held down and comparing the trigger key. In general, you should probably always use `SingleActivator` over `LogicalKeySet`.

Ah okay great thanks! That explains why I had weird delayed interactions with shortcuts before. I was aware of `SingleActivator` but I was using it for shortcuts actions that only use a single key (Escape, Enter, etc). I didn't realize it supported modifiers.

Okay I just tried to use `SingleActivator` and mostly it seems to be working fine but `command + enter` is giving me some problems. Not sure whether it's related to the original issues but I just switched back to `LogicalKeySet` for now.
Can you give more details about the problem?
Hmm, I'm having trouble reproing. I may be mistaken. The observed behavior was just that it seemed like the shortcut was not triggering as expected.

@chunhtai No, he can't :(
Tried to add semantics to the widget to solve the issue. Works only "value" parameter in semantics (hint or label appium didn't see). And add "value" is not a solution, because during input appium add that value too.
It seems to me Appium would need to add support for hinttext, are they using UIAutomator underneath to query the data?

@chunhtai 
Yep, they use uiautomator. But before we updated flutter version evrithing  were good. Maybe u can try to fix problem on your side?

That is unfortunately. The label/hint was put into hinttext in purpose. It was required for Talkback and VoiceAccess to work properly.

Previously we put them in value, and voiceaccess would think the label is part of the text that are already entered in the textfield. That would cause it to reenter the label to the textfield whenever user said a word.

Note: we are merely following how native android textfield sets the accessibility properties. So this is not a flutter specific issue I think.

The real fix would be on the UIAutomator to be able to spot hinttext. We would have to reach out to them for more insight
Hi. Created an issue for UIAutomator2
This issue is missing a priority label. Please set a priority label when adding the `triaged-design` label.

Workaround is to wrap TextField with another Semantics widget and create distinct SemanticsNode in semantics tree by setting container: true and explicitChildNodes: true. 

Code for reference:

```
Semantics(
  label: "Enter email address",
  container: true,
  explicitChildNodes: true,
  child: const TextField(),
);
```
This worked for Android and iOS, and Appium was able to select it on both platforms. This is how entering the text via Appium test looked like

```
await driver.$('~Enter email address').click();
await driver.sendKeys(process.env.TEST_USERNAME.split(''));
```
Source: https://stackoverflow.com/a/79609911/1274643

issues.title: Missing SemanticsNode of TextFormField's validator text when using `tester.pump`
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

I found this odd behaviour in testing `TextFormField` validation. 

I created a small app with one `TextFormField` and one submit `ElevatedButton`. When you submit with empty `TextFormField`, you'll get an `errorText` (of `InputDecoration` of `TextFormField`) saying "validation error".

I wrote a test for that by pumping the app widget, then tapping submit and doing either `pump` or `pumpAndSettle` then checking if "validation error" exists both on screen and in semantics.

The test passes for finding "validation error" on screen but the issue is finding semantics does not pass when using `pump` and it does not show in semantics tree. Although it passes using `pumpAndSettle` but documentation discourages from using `pumpAndSettle` in general.

Is it a bug? Is `pump` suppose to show `errorText` on screen but not on semantics tree?

### Expected results

When I use `tap` then `pump`, test `find.bySemanticsLabel` should pass for the `errorText` cases and `label: "validation error"` should be displayed in the semantics tree

### Actual results

I get this error:
```
Expected: exactly one matching candidate
  Actual: _ElementPredicateWidgetFinder:<Found 0 widgets with element matching predicate: []>
   Which: means none were found but one was expected
```

Also `SemanticsNode#6` in the logs below does not show `label: "validation error"`

issues.title: `TextFormField` do not disabled validation when set AutovalidateMode.disabled and Form parent has `autovalidateMode` != AutovalidateMode.disabled
issues.body:
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the [existing issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues)
- [X] I have read the [guide to filing a bug](https://flutter.dev/docs/resources/bug-reports)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run code sample
2. Write text into `TextFormField`
3. Error: Show error even `autovalidateMode` on TextFormField is disabled

### Expected results

When `TextFormField` has the property `autovalidateMode` with value **AutovalidateMode.disabled**, the field won't show the error, event with property `autovalidateMode` with **AutovalidateMode.always** or **AutovalidateMode.onUserInteraction** on `Form` parent widget

This would be _follow the Flutter's inherited widget convention were the innermost takes priority_ (https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/107350#issuecomment-1295694555)

### Actual results

When `Form` parent widget has property `autovalidateMode` with **AutovalidateMode.always** or **AutovalidateMode.onUserInteraction** and `TextFormField` has the property `autovalidateMode` with value **AutovalidateMode.disabled**; the `TextFormField` validate after the first user validation, like **AutovalidateMode.onUserInteraction**

### Code sample

<details><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
import 'package:flutter/material.dart';

void main() {
  runApp(const MyApp());
}

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget {
  const MyApp({super.key});

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return MaterialApp(
      title: 'Flutter Demo',
      theme: ThemeData(
        primarySwatch: Colors.blue,
      ),
      home: const MyHomePage(title: 'Flutter Demo Home Page'),
    );
  }
}

class MyHomePage extends StatefulWidget {
  const MyHomePage({super.key, required this.title});

  final String title;

  @override
  State<MyHomePage> createState() => _MyHomePageState();
}

class _MyHomePageState extends State<MyHomePage> {
  final _formKey = GlobalKey<FormState>();

  @override
  Widget build(BuildContext context) {
    return Scaffold(
      appBar: AppBar(
        title: Text(widget.title),
      ),
      body: Center(
        child: Padding(
          padding: const EdgeInsets.all(24.0),
          child: Column(
            mainAxisAlignment: MainAxisAlignment.center,
            children: <Widget>[
              Form(
                autovalidateMode: AutovalidateMode.always,
                child: TextFormField(
                  autovalidateMode: AutovalidateMode.disabled,
                  validator: (value) =>
                      value == null || value.length < 4 ? 'Not valid' : null,
                ),
              ),
            ],
          ),
        ),
      ),
      floatingActionButton: FloatingActionButton(
        onPressed: () => _formKey.currentState!.validate(),
        tooltip: 'Validate',
        child: const Icon(Icons.check),
      ),
    );
  }
}
```

</details>

### Additional information

1. Maybe related with #107350 and the linked PR #120730
2. I think that this error is because if not set `autovalidateMode` or set **AutovalidateMode.disabled**, is the same behavior. Can see in this line https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/9241329b4669a468e7e11ef1e2f6cbd23cd2098b/packages/flutter/lib/src/widgets/form.dart#L341
3. With currently behavior and definition, if by default `autovalidateMode` on FormField is disabled, the `autovalidateMode` property on `Form`parent never would be works because `FormField` property has priority with respect to `Form`

As mentioned in this issue description, fixing this by checking if the `TextFormField.autovalidateMode` is `AutovalidateMode.disabled` is not possible because `AutovalidateMode.disabled` is the default value (so any text form field wrapped by a form won't be validated with such a change).
Adding a new validation mode is probably the way to go.

issues.title: Textfield focus can become unattainable on an embedded flutter application in Safari
issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

The minimal code to reproduce the issue can be found [here](https://github.com/pearlcertification/safari_embedded_bug).

Steps to reproduce:
- Run the application and load the URL in Safari
- Click in the text field and type something
- Keystrokes will update the contents of the text field
- Click outside the embedded widget
- Click in the text field again and type something
- Keystrokes will _not_ update the contents of the text field

You can get around the issue by clicking inside the embedded application (but not in the text field) and then clicking back in the text field. After that, the text field contents will again update on each keystroke.

### Expected results

Typing in a text field should always update the contents of that text field in an embedded Flutter application (or any Flutter application).

### Actual results

After following the steps to reproduce, the text field no longer updates while the user is typing.

issues.title: IME lifecycle not working well in TextField
issues.body:
Hello! I am facing an issue when running my Android keyboard service in my Flutter app (or any Flutter apps), I found several issues opened on TextField but none matched my exact situation so I will try to detail it here:

## Steps to Reproduce
Android version: 9
Debugging an Android IME in any Flutter app, typing words and spaces in a `TextField`

**Expected results:**
The IME should follow a normal lifecycle

**Actual results:**
The IME `onStartInput` is called whenever a space is sent. 

I can try to provide more details if needed! Thanks!!
comments:
Hi @pierre225, thanks for filing the issue. 

I have a couple of questions

- Is this issue on only android 9?
- is the issue only when you press space? 
- what is the behaviour on in a native android app?
- How is the current behaviour affecting your app?

Please also provide the output of `flutter doctor -v`. 

Thank you

Hi @danagbemava-nc 

- This issue isn't only on Android 9, I reproduced on my Pixel Android 12
- Yes only when I press space (After a word is typed)
- On a native app, the lifecycle is normal, `onStartInput` is only called when the keyboard restarts
- This is affecting my keyboard because I have some logic in the `onStartInput` which should only be called when focusing a textfield once

I don't think it is super relevant to show you my Flutter doctor as this is happening in any Flutter apps, I tried on Reflectly
I tried building a simple app with Flutter beta 2.9.0-0.1.pre and it is still happening

One thing I noticed is that it is not happening when specifying some `keyboardType` to the TextField. For example it is not happening with `TextInputType.emailAddress` or `TextInputType.visiblePassword`.
But it is happening with `TextInputType.text` (Which is the default one?)

Let me know if I can provide more information, and thank you for the help :)

The output of `flutter doctor -v` is always relevant, for posterity. 

Please provide a sample that shows how your `onStartInput` logic is being affected and if possible a sample that shows the native behavior so we can confirm this. 

Please provide any relevant logs as well. 

Thank you

It may be easier to understand my issue with the lifecycle doc. Mi issue happens on the `onStartInput` it is triggered by the TextField after a space is sent while it should only be triggered when the context is changing.
 ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10887684/146745623-6d207710-8c98-41ce-aa5a-a95262603061.png)

Hi @pierre225, thanks for the illustration. 

Please provide a sample with steps to reproduce. 

Thank you

Hi @danagbemava-nc

I recorded a video in my Flutter app with 2 different TextField. I also added a toast message when onStartInput is triggered.
As you can see with a text TextField, onStartInput is triggered after each words

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10887684/146768899-df3be0aa-3a1d-4db3-a62e-75e9f8464f7d.mp4

Hi @pierre225, 

Please provide either steps to reproduce or a complete minimal reproducible code sample to make this issue actionable. 

Thank you

The Android text input plugin implementation calls [`InputMethodManager.restartInput`](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/view/inputmethod/InputMethodManager#restartInput(android.view.View)) whenever the composing region of the text is changed by the flutter framework (as opposed to changed by the IME), and that's most likely what's causing the `onStartInput` invocations you're seeing. Gboard's English keyboard for example removes the composing region when you enter a space but that probably shouldn't trigger a restart, as the composing region change is issued by the IME not the flutter framework.

Flutter text input formatters is the most common reason that IME restarts are requested, because they change the content of the text field and thus need to inform the IME of the change. But I'm not seeing an explicit text input formatter used in your repro so that's probably not the case. I'll look into this when I get a chance.

Hi @pierre225, 

How are you using the `KeyboardService` in your flutter project? 

I'm not entirely sure how to listen for the IME events in flutter.

I am still unsure of how I should solve this though, is the call of ` InputMethodManager.restartInput ` supposed to happen when the composing region is changed? Because it is not happening when updating the composing region in a classic Android EditText right?
I feel like the ` InputMethodManager.restartInput ` is also triggered when updating the composing region from the IME.
I am unsure where I can find the Flutter TextInput calling a restart of the IME?

Hi @danagbemava-nc 

The KeyboardService is an Android service, it is an external app/service installed on my android device to display a keyboard.
Then I use this service to write in Flutter's TextInputs

> Hi @LongCatIsLooong
> 
> Thank you for your detailed answer, it helps to understand my issue a bit better! I am still unsure of how I should solve this though, is the call of `InputMethodManager.restartInput` supposed to happen when the composing region is changed? Because it is not happening when updating the composing region in a classic Android EditText right? I feel like the `InputMethodManager.restartInput` is also triggered when updating the composing region from the IME. I am unsure where I can find the Flutter TextInput calling a restart of the IME?

The Android `TextInputConnection` documentation says the editor should call `InputMethodManager.restartInput` when the editor changes the content so that's basically what the android text input plugin does (but only when the composing region is changed by the framework). It shouldn't be called when the IME changes the composing region.

This issue was assigned to @bleroux but has had no status updates in a long time. To remove any ambiguity about whether the issue is being worked on, the assignee was removed.

@justinmc

**Summary**

The use case looks valid to me (despite not being part of M3 spec) but it can not be achieved by relying on `InputDecoration.alignLabelWithHint`.

**Explanation**

The default value of `InputDecoration.alignLabelWithHint` is false.
So I don’t think this issue could be fix by expecting that `alignLabelWithHint` being false should not align the label and the hint horizontally, because it would be a huge breaking change : the default `InputDecorator` for filled text field won’t be compliant with the M3 spec (the compliant rendering would require `InputDecoration.alignLabelWithHint` to be set to true).

The reason is that `alignLabelWithHint` was initially intended for vertical alignment only. See https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/24993 which introduced `alignLabelWithHint` parameter.
Unfortunately, https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/115409 relied on this parameter for the horizontal alignment of outlined text field. In this case, it was not a breaking change because by default it is expected that label and hint are not aligned and defaulting to false was possible. After that PR, one can set `alignLabelWithHint`  to true if the non default alignment is expected. It works for outlined decoration but it can't work for filled decoration.

If we had `InputDecorator.filled` and `InputDecorator.outlined` constructors and users were relying on these constructors, it would have been possible to set different default values for `alignLabelWithHint`. But that’s not the case.

If we consider adding a new parameter it should reflect differently for filled and outlined text fields. It would have to be a parameter that reflects that the label could be aligned in a non compliant way, which means we could default to false for both filled and outlined.

**Alternative**

`InputDecorator` supports two different positioning logic for the label:
- Filled text field: label painted inside the container above the input text
- Outline text field: label painted on the container border

The implementation currently choose the positioning based on `InputDecoration.border.isOutlined`.
Currently a possible, hacky?, solution for this problem is to create a subclass of `InputBorder` with a positioning logic somewhat similar to the filled one but with `InputBorder.isOutlined` returning true.
See [this comment](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/110425#issuecomment-1919504402) and the [associated code](https://gist.github.com/Vanchel/d473ef4204ab54b6203020bc4f43c994).
